Services¶

auth¶

class src.services.auth.Hash[source]¶

Bases: object

Password hashing utilities using bcrypt.

get_password_hash(password: str) → str[source]¶

Return a bcrypt hash of password.

verify_password(plain_password: str, hashed_password: str) → bool[source]¶

Return True if plain_password matches the bcrypt hash.

async src.services.auth.create_access_token(data: dict, expires_delta: int | None = None) → str[source]¶

Create a short-lived JWT access token.

Parameters:

  • data – Payload dict; must include sub (username).

  • expires_delta – Override TTL in seconds; defaults to settings value.

Returns:

Encoded JWT string.

src.services.auth.create_email_token(data: dict) → str[source]¶

Create a 7-day JWT for email verification.

Parameters:

data – Payload dict; must include sub (email).

Returns:

Encoded JWT string.

src.services.auth.create_password_reset_token(email: str) → str[source]¶

Create a 1-hour JWT for password reset.

Parameters:

email – The user’s email address embedded as sub.

Returns:

Encoded JWT string.

async src.services.auth.create_refresh_token(data: dict, expires_delta: int | None = None) → str[source]¶

Create a long-lived JWT refresh token.

Parameters:

  • data – Payload dict; must include sub (username).

  • expires_delta – Override TTL in seconds; defaults to settings value.

Returns:

Encoded JWT string.

async src.services.auth.get_current_user(token: str = Depends(dependency=<fastapi.security.oauth2.OAuth2PasswordBearer object>, use_cache=True, scope=None), db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None)) → User[source]¶

FastAPI dependency: validate JWT access token and return the current user.

Checks Redis cache first; falls back to DB on cache miss and re-populates cache.

Raises:

HTTPException 401 – If the token is invalid or the user does not exist.

async src.services.auth.get_email_from_token(token: str) → str[source]¶

Decode an email-verification JWT and return the email.

Raises:

HTTPException 422 – If the token is invalid or missing sub.

async src.services.auth.invalidate_user_cache(username: str) → None[source]¶

Remove a user’s cache entry from Redis.

Call this after any mutation (password change, role update, email confirm, etc.).

src.services.auth.require_role(required_role: UserRole)[source]¶

Return a FastAPI dependency that enforces the given minimum role.

Parameters:

required_role – The role the current user must have.

Returns:

An async dependency callable.

async src.services.auth.verify_password_reset_token(token: str) → str[source]¶

Decode a password-reset JWT and return the email.

Raises:

HTTPException 400 – If the token is invalid, expired, or has the wrong type.

users¶

class src.services.users.UserService(db: AsyncSession)[source]¶

Bases: object

Business logic layer for user operations.

async confirmed_email(email: str) → None[source]¶

Confirm a user’s email address.

async create_user(body: UserCreate) → User[source]¶

Create a new user, auto-generating a Gravatar avatar if possible.

async get_user_by_email(email: str) → User | None[source]¶

Return a user by email.

async get_user_by_username(username: str) → User | None[source]¶

Return a user by username.

async update_avatar_url(email: str, url: str) → User[source]¶

Update a user’s avatar URL.

async update_password(user: User, hashed_password: str) → None[source]¶

Update a user’s password hash and revoke the refresh token.

async update_refresh_token(user: User, token: str | None) → None[source]¶

Store or revoke a user’s refresh token.

email¶

async src.services.email.send_email(email: EmailStr, username: str, host: str) → None[source]¶

Send an email verification message to a newly registered user.

Parameters:

  • email – Recipient’s email address.

  • username – Recipient’s display name.

  • host – Base URL of the application, used to build the confirmation link.

async src.services.email.send_password_reset_email(email: EmailStr, username: str, host: str) → None[source]¶

Send a password reset link to the user.

Parameters:

  • email – Recipient’s email address.

  • username – Recipient’s display name.

  • host – Base URL of the application, used to build the reset link.

redis_cache¶

async src.services.redis_cache.cache_delete(key: str) → None[source]¶

Delete a key from Redis.

async src.services.redis_cache.cache_get(key: str) → Any | None[source]¶

Retrieve and deserialize a JSON value from Redis, or return None on miss.

async src.services.redis_cache.cache_set(key: str, value: Any, ttl: int) → None[source]¶

Serialize value to JSON and store it in Redis with the given TTL.

async src.services.redis_cache.get_redis() → Redis[source]¶

Return a shared async Redis client, creating it on first call.

src.services.redis_cache.user_cache_key(username: str) → str[source]¶

Return the Redis cache key for a given username.

upload_file¶

Cloudinary file upload service.

class src.services.upload_file.UploadFileService(cloud_name: str, api_key: int, api_secret: str)[source]¶

Bases: object

Handles avatar uploads to Cloudinary.

static upload_file(file, username: str) → str[source]¶

Upload a file to Cloudinary and return a cropped 250x250 URL.

Parameters:

  • file – An UploadFile object from FastAPI.

  • username – Used as the Cloudinary public ID path segment.

Returns:

URL of the uploaded and transformed image.
